sections.paragraph:
ἀπόρροια, ας, ἡ (ἀπορρέω; since Xen., Hell. 5, 2, 5, also Wsd 7:25; Philo, Ath.) lit. ‘a flowing off, a stream’; esp. emanation αὐτὴ ἡ ἀ. τῆς ἐννοίας she, the emanation itself of the (divine) mind Ox 1081 30f (SJCh 90, 7f)=Otero I no. 3 p. 83 (cp. Herm. Fgm. 23, 3 al. and Rtzst., Poim., p. 16, n. 4).—DELG s.v. ῥέω C.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

esp. esp. = especially

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

SJCh SJCh = Sophia Jesu Christi—List 1

Otero Otero = AdeSantosO., Los Evangelios Apocrifos—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Rtzst., Poim. Rtzst., Poim. = Poimandres

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 119
